CREATE TABLE employees (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), salary FLOAT);
CREATE TABLE departments (dept_id INT PRIMARY KEY, dept_name VARCHAR(100));
SELECT name FROM employees WHERE salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees);
SELECT e.name, d.dept_name FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON d.dept_id = e.id WHERE e.salary > 50000;
SELECT e.name FROM employees e WHERE e.id IN (SELECT id FROM employees WHERE salary >= (SELECT MIN(salary) FROM employees));
SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM employees WHERE salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees));
SELECT e.name, COUNT(d.dept_id) FROM employees e LEFT JOIN departments d ON d.dept_id = e.id GROUP BY e.name HAVING COUNT(d.dept_id) > 0;
SELECT e.name, d.dept_name FROM employees e FULL OUTER JOIN departments d ON e.id = d.dept_id WHERE e.name IS NOT NULL OR d.dept_name IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE projects (project_id INT PRIMARY KEY, project_name VARCHAR(100), budget FLOAT);
INSERT INTO projects VALUES (1, 'Project A', 120000.00), (2, 'Project B', 150000.00);
SELECT e.name, p.project_name FROM employees e JOIN projects p ON p.project_id = e.id WHERE p.budget > (SELECT AVG(budget) FROM projects);
SELECT e.name FROM employees e WHERE e.salary BETWEEN 40000 AND 70000 ORDER BY e.salary DESC LIMIT 5;
WITH HighSalary AS (SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary > 50000) SELECT name FROM HighSalary ORDER BY salary DESC;
